🏆 League Leaders

—
Every starter on today's slate + hot/cold teams. Window: L10 (last 10 played games).
🔗 How League Leaders feeds the PP-edge engine
Every factor below is already wired into v2_scorer.py:
  • Statcast Factor (SC): xBA, hard-hit%, K% over L14 days → contributes to combined_factor
  • L10 Factor: per-game per-stat rates over last 10 played games → contributes to combined_factor
  • Combined (ALL): SC × L10 (clamped 0.85–1.18) — the visible "heat" composite
  • PP-edge impact: when a player-prop pick fires on this player, SC + L10 are each softened by W=0.45, then compounded onto true_prob. The "PP" badge on each row shows the exact effective edge contribution.
  • Cold side: factors < 1.0 SUBTRACT from true_prob — engine dampens edges on slumping players automatically.
HOT — Top 15 — Overall
COLD — Bottom 15 — Overall

📐 Stake Recommender + Slip Tools

—

tier-capped · variance/sample dampened · hard cap 5% bankroll/pick

Top edges · LOCK stack · NRFI 2-leg · Lottery
🧠 Slip insights (live from Bet Builder)

🧮 Live Model State

factor stack + calibration params
📚 How the AI math works (reference)
  • · Stake Recommender: ¼-Kelly stake_pct = (b·p − q) / b · ¼ · tier_mult · 1/variance_adj · sample_dampener · capped at 5% bankroll
  • · Auto-build: scores picks by (edge_pp × 10 + tier_weight); takes top N for each risk profile
  • · Pre-baked Parlays: rule-based filters (LOCK only, NRFI top-2, longshots ≥ +150)
  • · AI Insights: parlay true_prob × correlation discount (1.15× per same-game leg pair), EV = stake × (true_prob × payout − loss_prob)
  • · Calibration pipeline: base_prob (American→prob) × ∏(softened factors with W) → true_prob (clamped 1–99%) → edge_pp = (true_prob − base_prob) × 100, clamped ±15pp
  • · AI Learner: reads settled picks + factor metadata weekly, aggregates lift per factor, recommends weight tweaks. Never auto-applies — operator reviews + hand-edits.

    🌙 Late Slate

    Picks fired after 13:00 ET · isolated from morning slate
    Late-window scouts surface their candidates here instead of overwriting your morning slate. The morning 5 LIVE / 25 PAPER are locked the moment you confirm them; late picks live separately so you can review without losing your earlier work.

    🎯 Late-Window LIVE Candidates

    —
    Engine's top recommendations from the most recent post-13:00 ET scout. Confirm via Notion if you want to bet them — they won't replace your morning slate.

    📋 Late-Window PAPER Candidates

    —

    ℹ️ How this works

    1. Morning lock fires ~11:30 ET — writes 5 LIVE + 25 PAPER as Pending-Unconfirmed.
    2. You confirm picks (flip to Pending) and place on FanDuel — those picks are now sticky.
    3. After 13:00 ET, evening scouts produce a new ranking. Picks NOT already in your confirmed slate show up here as CHALLENGERS with their new rank (1 = most confident).
    4. To swap: confirm the challenger in Notion (Result → Pending), then manually Auto-Archive the morning pick you're replacing.
    5. To deny: leave the challenger as Pending-Unconfirmed — it rolls off without firing. Your morning slate is untouched either way.
    🧪
    Phase 1 · read-only evaluation

    Backtest Lab

    Does a 3-lens ensemble (edge + form + situational) pick winners better than pure-edge ranking?
    The 3 lenses how scores are combined
    1
    Edge (current math)
    Risk-Adjusted Edge — the engine's edge_pp, the gap between model probability and market implied probability. Pure quantitative output, no narrative.
    Weight ≈ 50%
    2
    Form (player)
    Hot/cold batter rank from League Leaders. +20 if top-10 hot, +10 if 11-25, −10 if cold top-10. Captures recent-game momentum the edge math underweights.
    Weight ≈ 30%
    3
    Situational (matchup)
    Opposing pitcher hot/cold rank. +25 vs cold top-5 starter, +15 vs cold 6-10, −20 vs hot top-3. Captures the soft-spot starters the market underprices.
    Weight ≈ 20%
    Aggregate results across all backtested days · same picks, two rankings
    Day-by-day edge vs lens for each settled date
    Top divergences picks the lens ranked very differently from edge
    Today's active picks · lens-scored live + paper, pending + unconfirmed
    📒 Player directory recent track record on today's slate players
    For each player in today's slate, recent pick outcomes from the settled archive. Read it as: green W rate + positive ROI = trustworthy edge; red ROI = engine has been getting these wrong.
    How to use this tab your day-to-day workflow with the backtest
    Each morning
    Refresh this tab. Yesterday's settled picks have been re-graded by the lens overnight. Glance at the Δ ROI on the Top-5 cohort — that's the headline number for whether the lens is earning its keep.
    During the day
    Cross-reference with Most Confident Plays. When a pick has high edge AND would have been a top-5 lens pick, that's an extra-conviction signal. Use this tab as a sanity check, not a betting trigger.
    Once a week
    Look at Top divergences — picks where lens disagreed most with edge. If lens disagreement consistently predicts losers, the form/situational lenses are doing real work. If random, they're noise — adjust weights or kill the lens.
    Decision threshold
    Advance to Phase 2 (side-by-side display) when the Top-5 Δ ROI is ≥+10pp across ≥14 days. Until then, this tab is observation only — the engine stays edge-ranked.
    🤖

    —
    What it does · reads backtest + Notion picks + journal → spots tier drift, factor imbalances, strategy promote/demote candidates
    What it can't do · edit code, write Notion, change weights without operator confirm
    Kill switch · TG /killlearner · revive with /revivelearner
    ⚠ Caveats read before drawing conclusions
    • Small sample. A trustworthy verdict needs ≥14 days of data. The current numbers are directional, not definitive.
    • Top-25 doesn't improve. When the cohort includes the entire settled pool, re-ranking doesn't change which picks were bet. Lens benefit is concentrated at top-5 and top-10.
    • Concentration risk. One hot player on a great day can dominate the lens advantage for that day. Single-day variance is high until sample grows.
    • This page is READ-ONLY. No picks are written to Notion from this analysis. No bankroll math is altered. The production engine uses edge-rank as today.
    Rollout phases what comes after we trust the data
    0
    Shadow scoring · running now
    Lens scores computed on every candidate, persisted alongside RAE. Nothing acts on them yet.
    1
    Backtest evaluation · this page
    Apply lens retroactively to settled days. Compare ROI: lens-rank vs edge-rank.
    2
    Side-by-side display · after ≥14 days
    Show lens top-25 next to edge top-25 on Today tab. Operator can confirm picks from either view. No engine change yet.
    3
    Lens as primary ranker · if backtest holds
    Engine uses lens score as primary sort key for the slate. Math RAE remains the floor. Kill switch via env var.
    4
    Calibration loop · ongoing
    Per-night logging of which lens predicted winners. Monthly weight retuning based on evidence.
    5
    LLM narrative scorer · later, only if 4 holds
    Claude reads each candidate's research card → score + 1-sentence rationale. Bounded influence (±20%), daily budget cap.
    🤖 AI Decipher plain-English: what every +/− number means + dynamic insights from today's data ▾
    RAE / Risk-Adjusted Edge — the engine's signed conviction score. RAE = (edge_raw × confidence^1.5) / variance_adjustment. Higher = more conviction. The current production ranker.
    edge_pp — percentage-point gap between model probability and FD implied probability. A pick at +6.0pp means the engine thinks the prop hits 6 percentage points more often than the market is pricing.
    Lens score — composite ranking number: edge_pp + form_adjust + situational_adjust. Same engine, three perspectives blended into one.
    form_adjust — adjustment based on the player's recent batting form rank. +20 if hot top-10, +10 if 11-25, −10 if cold top-10.
    situational_adjust — adjustment based on opposing pitcher's hot/cold rank. +25 vs cold top-5 starter (favorable), +15 vs cold 6-10, −20 vs hot top-3.
    ROI — return on investment. Net P/L divided by total stake, expressed as percent. +5% ROI = you make 5¢ per dollar wagered, long-run.
    Δ ROI (pp) — difference between two ROI numbers, in percentage points. +62pp means lens beat edge by 62 percentage points of ROI — huge if real, suspicious if from a small sample.
    Top-N cohort — the picks ranked #1 through #N by that metric. Top-5 = the 5 highest-conviction picks of the day; Top-25 = the full slate.
    Tier — engine bucket: LOCK > STRONG > GOOD > SIDE > WATCH. Determines stake size (LOCK = 2u, GOOD = 1u, etc.).
    Pending vs Pending-Unconfirmed — Pending = operator confirmed and placed; Pending-Unconfirmed = engine wrote it but operator hasn't acted. Only Pending counts toward bankroll lock + "fired" stat.
    Lens verdict — qualitative summary of the lens score: loves (≥30), likes (15-29), neutral (−5 to +14), cautious (−15 to −6), skeptical (≤−16).
    Where to look first
    Hero stat strip at the very top → 4 cohort-level Δ ROIs (Top 5, 10, 25, all). Your eye should land on Top-5 — that's the LIVE-Pending zone where lens performance matters most for real money.
    What "good" looks like
    Top-5 Δ ROI consistently +10pp or more across 14+ days = the lens is earning its place. Below +5pp = noise. Negative = lens is hurting; revert weights.
    Why Top-25 is usually 0
    When the lens and edge see the same candidate pool, reranking the SAME 25 picks doesn't change W-L outcomes — only the ORDER changes. Lens advantage shows up at Top-5 because that's where you DROP picks. To get a Top-25 lens advantage, widen the candidate pool above 25 so reranking actually changes selection.
    Reading the Top divergences row
    Each row shows a pick where edge and lens disagreed strongly. The Δ column is signed: + means lens upgraded it (lens liked it more), − means lens downgraded it. Then check Result: upgrade + WIN = lens was right; downgrade + LOSS = lens was right. Random outcomes = lens is noise.
    Reading the Active picks table
    For each Pending or Pending-Unconfirmed pick, the lens column shows the engine's real-time conviction beyond pure edge. A lens loves (≥30) on an unconfirmed pick is a strong signal to confirm. A lens skeptical on a confirmed pick is a flag — consider why.
    Reading the Player directory
    This panel only lists players who appear in today's slate AND had prior settled picks. Win rate > 60% with positive ROI = the engine reliably picks this player. Negative ROI despite reasonable W rate = engine wins on him but at odds that don't compensate — risky. Negative ROI + low W rate = engine is wrong about this player; ignore his next pick or reduce stake.

    📱 Telegram

    Bot ID: —
    Chat: SportsPicks channel
    Listener daemon: active (polls every ~25s)
    Commands from your phone:
    /status — bankrolls, last fire, pause state
    /picks — today's open picks
    /recap — push yesterday's recap
    /pause — emergency stop (next lock won't fire)
    /resume — clear pause
    /help — list all commands

    ⚠ Disclaimer: Outputs are engine research, not financial advice. Sports betting involves risk of total loss. Past performance does not guarantee future results.
